title: Stats: Normal Distribution and Z-Scores
content:
### Day 83: Stats: Normal Distribution and Z-Scores

**Normal Distribution:** Bell curve where 68% within 1σ, 95% within 2σ, 99.7% within 3σ.

**Z-Score:** How many std devs from mean: z = (x - μ) / σ
- z > 2 or z < -2 → potential outlier
codeExample:
# Day 83: Stats: Normal Distribution and Z-Scores
import numpy as np
np.random.seed(42)
data = np.random.normal(100, 15, 1000)  # IQ scores
print(f"Mean: {np.mean(data):.1f}, Std: {np.std(data):.1f}")
print(f"Within 1σ: {np.sum(np.abs(data - np.mean(data)) < np.std(data))/len(data)*100:.1f}%")

# Z-score
def z_score(x, data):
    return (x - np.mean(data)) / np.std(data)

for val in [85, 100, 130, 145]:
    z = z_score(val, data)
    print(f"  IQ {val}: z={z:.2f} {'⚠️ outlier' if abs(z) > 2 else ''}")
practiceQuestions: When is a z-score considered an outlier?
Why is normal distribution important in ML?
